subject: Statistics
title: The Chi-Squared Distribution
description: A 60-second lesson on The Chi-Squared Distribution.

hook.text: Is a die fair? Is a survey result too far from expectations? Chi-squared answers both.

buildup.text: The chi-squared distribution measures how far observed counts deviate from expected counts.

discovery.text: Square each difference, divide by expected, add them up. That sum follows a chi-squared distribution.

twist.text: It only works with count data. Using it on continuous measurements gives meaningless results.

climax.text: Genetics, marketing surveys, and election analysis all use chi-squared to test patterns.

punchline.text: How far is observed from expected? Chi-squared tells.

quiz.question: What type of data does the chi-squared test require?
